title: Santa Cruz Basilica
tagline: Santa Cruz Basilica — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: The Santa Cruz Cathedral Basilica, also known as Kotta Palli or Kottepalli, is located in Fort Kochi, Kochi.
overview.description: The Santa Cruz Cathedral Basilica, also known as Kotta Palli or Kottepalli, is located in Fort Kochi, Kochi. It is one of the thirty-four basilicas in India and one of nine in Kerala. This heritage edifice of Kerala is renowned for its Indo-European and Gothic architectural style. It serves as the cathedral church of the Diocese of Cochin.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Cochin
topPlaces.description: The Diocese of Cochin (Latin: Diœcesis Coccinensis) is a Latin Church diocese of the Catholic Church in Cochin, Kerala, India. A constituent of the sui iuris Latin Church, the diocese was established in 1557 after the domination of the Portuguese-speaking missionaries.

topPlaces.name: Santa Cruz HSS, Fort Kochi
topPlaces.description: Santa Cruz Higher Secondary School was established in 1888. It currently has higher secondary level status.

topPlaces.name: Fort Kochi
topPlaces.description: Fort Kochi ( KOH-chee, Malayalam: [cò:çi kō:tā]; Cochin Portuguese: Cochim de Baixo, European Portuguese: [kuˈʃĩ ðɨ ˈβaʃu]), formerly also known as Fort Cochin, is a region of Kochi city in Kerala, India. Fort Kochi takes its name from the Fort Manuel of Kochi, the first European fort on Indian soil, controlled by the Portuguese East Indies.

topPlaces.name: Vasco House, Kochi
topPlaces.description: Vasco House is one of the oldest surviving Portuguese residences in Fort Kochi, located in the city of Kochi, in the Indian state of Kerala. It is believed by some sources to have once been the residence of the Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama.

topPlaces.name: Church of Saint Francis, Kochi
topPlaces.description: Saint Francis Church, in Fort Kochi, Kochi, originally built in 1503, is one of the oldest European churches in India and has historical significance as a witness to the European colonial ambitions in the subcontinent. The Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama died in Kochi in 1524 when he was on his third visit to India.

topPlaces.name: Old Harbour House
topPlaces.description: Old Harbour House is a building in the center of Fort Kochi, part of the city of Kochi (formerly known as Cochin) in the district of Ernakulam in the state of Kerala (India). It was built during the Portuguese colonial period, almost completely rebuilt by the Dutch in the 17th century, and later modified by the British.

topPlaces.name: Fort Kochi Beach
topPlaces.description: Fort Kochi Beach is a beach along the Arabian Sea, situated in the Fort Kochi neighbourhood of the city of Kochi in Kerala, South India.

topPlaces.name: Indo-Portuguese Museum
topPlaces.description: The Indo-Portuguese Museum is a museum in Fort Kochi, Kerala, India.

items.note: The Diocese of Cochin (Latin: Diœcesis Coccinensis) is a Latin Church diocese of the Catholic Church in Cochin, Kerala, India. A constituent of the sui iuris Latin Church, the diocese was established in 1557 after the domination of the Portuguese-speaking missionaries.
Santa Cruz Higher Secondary School was established in 1888. It currently has higher secondary level status.

Fort Kochi ( KOH-chee, Malayalam: [cò:çi kō:tā]; Cochin Portuguese: Cochim de Baixo, European Portuguese: [kuˈʃĩ ðɨ ˈβaʃu]), formerly also known as Fort Cochin, is a region of Kochi city in Kerala, India. Fort Kochi takes its name from the Fort Manuel of Kochi, the first European fort on Indian soil, controlled by the Portuguese East Indies.

Vasco House is one of the oldest surviving Portuguese residences in Fort Kochi, located in the city of Kochi, in the Indian state of Kerala. It is believed by some sources to have once been the residence of the Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama.

Saint Francis Church, in Fort Kochi, Kochi, originally built in 1503, is one of the oldest European churches in India and has historical significance as a witness to the European colonial ambitions in the subcontinent. The Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama died in Kochi in 1524 when he was on his third visit to India.

Old Harbour House is a building in the center of Fort Kochi, part of the city of Kochi (formerly known as Cochin) in the district of Ernakulam in the state of Kerala (India). It was built during the Portuguese colonial period, almost completely rebuilt by the Dutch in the 17th century, and later modified by the British.
Fort Kochi Beach is a beach along the Arabian Sea, situated in the Fort Kochi neighbourhood of the city of Kochi in Kerala, South India.
The Indo-Portuguese Museum is a museum in Fort Kochi, Kerala, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Santa Cruz Basilica?
faq.a: The best time to visit Santa Cruz Basilica is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Santa Cruz Basilica?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (27 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (5 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Santa Cruz Basilica?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Santa Cruz Basilica at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Santa Cruz Basilica?
faq.a: Stays in Santa Cruz Basilica start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Santa Cruz Basilica?
faq.a: The top attractions are Diocese of Cochin, Santa Cruz HSS, Fort Kochi, Fort Kochi, Vasco House, Kochi, Church of Saint Francis, Kochi.
